Identity.Name: Pset_StingTags
Identity.Definition: The core 8-segment ISO 19650 tag carried by every taggable element. Each segment is a member of its corresponding STING enumeration (Discipline / System / Function / Product) or matches a code defined on a spatial entity (Location / Zone / Level). FullTag is the computed dash-separated rendering. PreviousTag and ModifiedAt support the tag-history audit trail; TokenLock locks individual segments against the auto-populator's overwrite pass.

Governance.StandardsBasis: STING tag grammar v5.0; ISO 19650-2 §5.3 element identification

Property.name: Discipline
Property.Definition: Discipline code — first segment of the 8-segment tag. Required from Stage 2 onward. References StingDisciplineCodes.

Property.name: Location
Property.Definition: Location / building code — second segment. References StingLocationCodes via the project's overlay, and must equal the LocationCode of the element's containing IfcBuilding (cross-entity rule LOC_MATCHES_BUILDING). Required from Stage 2.

Property.name: Zone
Property.Definition: Zone code — third segment. References StingZoneCodes. Must equal a ZoneCode on at least one IfcZone the element is assigned to via IfcRelAssignsToGroup (cross-entity rule ZONE_MATCHES_ASSIGNEDZONE). Required from Stage 3.

Property.name: Level
Property.Definition: Level / storey code — fourth segment. References StingLevelCodes and must equal the LevelCode of the element's containing IfcBuildingStorey (cross-entity rule LVL_MATCHES_STOREY). Required from Stage 2.

Property.name: System
Property.Definition: System-type code — fifth segment. References StingSystemCodes. Required from Stage 3 for MEP / FLS / structural elements; optional for architectural (default ARC). Must align with the IfcSystem the element belongs to when one exists.

Property.name: Function
Property.Definition: Function code — sixth segment. References StingFunctionCodes. Narrows the role of the element within its System (e.g. HVAC SUP vs RET vs EXH). Required from Stage 4 for MEP; optional otherwise.

Property.name: Product
Property.Definition: Product code — seventh segment. References StingProductCodes. Names the kind of element (AHU, FCU, DB, basin). Required from Stage 4 for tagged elements; optional for elements whose category is unambiguous via IFC class alone.

Property.name: Sequence
Property.Definition: Sequence number — eighth segment. Zero-padded 4-digit integer in canonical form (e.g. "0042"). Assigned by STING's TokenAutoPopulator + SEQ-counter sidecar; unique within (Discipline, System, Level) tuple. Required from Stage 3.

Property.name: FullTag
Property.Definition: Computed dash-separated rendering of the 8 segments — e.g. "M-BLD1-Z01-L02-HVAC-SUP-AHU-0042". DERIVED, not authoritative — host plugins compute on read from the 8 source segments; storing this property is a convenience for legacy consumers but is not required (when present, STING validators verify it matches the computed form).

Property.name: PreviousTag
Property.Definition: The element's previous FullTag value before the most recent retag. Audit history; populated by the auto-tagger when overwriting. Cleared on element creation.

Property.name: ModifiedAt
Property.Definition: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of the last tag-segment modification. Used for stale-detection and synchronisation between host plugins and Planscape Server.

Property.name: TokenLock
Property.Definition: Comma-separated list of segment names locked against auto-population. Values: DISC, LOC, ZONE, LVL, SYS, FUNC, PROD, SEQ. Empty = no segments locked = auto-populator may overwrite all. Locked segments are preserved by every STING tag-write path.

Rule.Description: Discipline segment must be non-empty and a member of StingDisciplineCodes (excluding sentinel XX at Stage 3+).
Rule.RemediationInstruction: Open STING Tags panel, set Discipline (M / E / P / A / S / FP / LV / G / H / MG / RP).

Rule.Description: See Pset_StingSpatialCodes Rule LOC_MATCHES_BUILDING. Encoded as IDS specs 01a + 01b in sting-spatial-codes.ids, with STING-side equality closeout.
Rule.FailMessage: Element Location does not match a building's LocationCode.
Rule.RemediationInstruction: Verify the element is in the correct building, then resync LOC via STING.

Rule.Description: See Pset_StingSpatialCodes Rule LVL_MATCHES_STOREY. Encoded as IDS specs 02a + 02b in sting-spatial-codes.ids.
Rule.FailMessage: Element Level does not match the storey's LevelCode.
Rule.RemediationInstruction: Verify the element is on the correct storey, then resync LVL via STING.

Rule.Description: See Pset_StingSpatialCodes Rule ZONE_MATCHES_ASSIGNEDZONE. Encoded as IDS specs 03a + 03b in sting-spatial-codes.ids.
Rule.FailMessage: Element Zone is not the ZoneCode of any IfcZone it is assigned to.
Rule.RemediationInstruction: Assign the element to the correct IfcZone via STING, then set Zone token to match.

Rule.Description: If the element is a member of an IfcSystem (via IfcRelAssignsToGroup), the System token must align with the IfcSystem's classification (HVAC / electrical / plumbing / etc).
Rule.FailMessage: Element System token mismatches the IfcSystem it is assigned to.
Rule.RemediationInstruction: Resync System token from the assigned IfcSystem via STING's SystemParamPush command.

Rule.Description: Sequence must be unique within the (Discipline, System, Level) tuple. Two elements in the same DISC/SYS/LVL group cannot share SEQ.
Rule.RemediationInstruction: Run STING's RepairDuplicateSeq command to auto-assign a new unique SEQ.

Rule.Description: If FullTag is set, it must equal the dash-joined form of the 8 source segments. When absent, the host plugin computes on read.
Rule.FailMessage: Stored FullTag does not match the computed form of the source segments.
Rule.RemediationInstruction: Run STING's BuildTags command to refresh FullTag from source segments.

Rule.Description: The auto-populator must not modify any segment listed in TokenLock. Violations of this rule indicate a host plugin bug, not user error. BEHAVIOURAL ASSERTION: this rule cannot be verified from a static IFC snapshot — enforcement lives in each host plugin's tag-write path (Revit's TokenAutoPopulator + the equivalent in the Bonsai add-on). The IDS specification 19-TOKENLOCK in sting-tag-grammar.ids validates the CSV format of the TokenLock value when present, but cannot prove that the auto-populator respected it during a write.
Rule.FailMessage: A locked token was modified by auto-population.
Rule.RemediationInstruction: File bug against the host plugin; restore the locked value from PreviousTag.

Rule.Description: On retag (any segment change), PreviousTag and ModifiedAt must be updated. Enforces audit-trail completeness. BEHAVIOURAL ASSERTION: this rule cannot be verified from a single static IFC snapshot — it describes the host plugin's contract when a retag happens. Enforcement lives in the tag-write path; an audit-log entry pinned via PlanscapeAuditLog records each retag as the canonical evidence.
Rule.FailMessage: Tag changed without updating PreviousTag / ModifiedAt.
Rule.RemediationInstruction: Host plugin contract violation. Re-run tag write through the canonical write-path.
